title: Design optional LLM analysis over sanitized evidence only
description:
Context: LLM analysis is useful later, but it should be downstream of deterministic source checks and prompt-injection detection.
Scope: model prompt contracts, input constraints, schemas, confidence/uncertainty reporting.
Acceptance criteria:
- Define which reduced artifact fields an LLM may see for semantic review.
- Define outputs as structured findings with evidence references, uncertainty, and no autonomous trust decision.
- Include an escalation path for complex/high-risk commits and suspicious mailing-list context.
- Document that raw git/email content remains hostile data, not instructions.
Risk notes:
- Avoid opaque maliciousness scores; require explanations tied to deterministic evidence.

title: Model longitudinal author work-area and co-change baselines
description:
Context: Range review should eventually compare a change against historical norms, including which parts of the codebase an author usually touches and which files/path-prefixes usually change together in a commit. This can help flag compromised trusted developers, unusual subsystem expansion, and suspicious split-commit composition.
Scope: design baseline data model, deterministic local extraction from git history, storage/normalization, and reviewer-facing signals.
Acceptance criteria:
- Define historical author work-area summaries over configurable refs/windows, including path-prefix/subsystem distributions and unusual-current-range deltas.
- Define co-change summaries for files and path prefixes normally changed together, including stable pair counts and unusual pair/new coupling signals.
- Define privacy/secrets posture for public fixtures and local repo analysis; do not persist private repo metadata by default.
- Add fixture/golden strategy for multi-author and co-change baseline behavior.
Risk notes:
- Baselines are evidence, not verdicts; avoid overfitting to small history windows or labeling legitimate first-time work as malicious.
- Git history and commit metadata are hostile input; keep deterministic parsing and bounded output.

title: Parse a single mailing-list message into normalized facts
description:
Context: Mailing-list context remains important, but the Linux value probe suggests git-only merge/range review should become useful first. Email parsing is the deterministic hostile-input foundation for later lore/discussion correlation.
Scope: MIME/email parser, normalized message schema, prompt-injection/static-risk hints, and fixtures.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given raw RFC822 email or mbox entry, emit stable JSON with message-id, date, from, to/cc/list ids, subject, in-reply-to/references, plain-text body excerpt, patch/diff presence, and attachment metadata.
- Detect and flag prompt-injection language, executable-looking snippets, URLs/domains, and truncation.
- Preserve source references while treating all message content as hostile input.
- Include tests for multipart messages, patch emails, replies, malformed headers, and huge bodies.
Risk notes:
- Email bodies and headers are hostile content; reducers must bound and label content before model use. This should enrich already-useful git review rather than become a prerequisite for first value.
close_reason: Implemented bounded RFC822/mbox message parser with CLI, scorecard coverage, and hostile-input regression tests

title: Define testing strategy and fixture pyramid for source review
description:
Context: The project needs confidence that each iteration provides value without pretending to solve full malicious-change detection.
Scope: testing pyramid, fixture strategy, regression corpus, confidence metrics, CI gates.
Acceptance criteria:
- Define unit tests for schema validation, commit parsing, email parsing, prompt-injection hints, static rules, and review packet rendering.
- Define integration tests using synthetic git repos and local RFC822/mbox fixtures; no network required in default CI.
- Define golden review-packet fixtures for easy wins such as removed tests, CI/static-analysis weakening, suspicious executable additions, prompt-injection text, high-risk kernel paths, and oversized diffs.
- Define optional slow/smoke tests against public Raspberry Pi kernel/lore data that are not required for default PR CI.
- Define per-iteration value checks: each merged bead must add at least one observable reviewer signal, parser capability, or evidence traceability improvement.
Risk notes:
- Avoid coverage theater; high line coverage does not prove useful review quality.
notes: Starting concrete testing-strategy completion slice: make docs explicit enough to satisfy acceptance criteria, add lightweight doc guard tests, then close if gates pass.
close_reason: Completed concrete testing strategy: docs/testing-strategy.md now defines source-review fixture pyramid, unit coverage matrix, integration fixture rules, golden review-packet fixtures, optional Raspberry Pi/lore public-data smoke boundaries, and per-iteration value checks. Added doc guard test and README reference. Verification: pytest -q; scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh; ruff check .; mypy src all passed.

comments.author: operator
comments.text: Planning note from user: treat X->X+1 and X->X+n review as fundamentally similar because both are diffs. Range review should be first-class, not just per-commit aggregation: cumulative diffs can reveal patterns missed by individual commit review. Under the temporary simplifying assumption that only one author is malicious/compromised, author-linked patterns across short-term commits may help infer longer-term intent.

comments.author: operator
comments.text: Progress: added first range-level evidence slice. review-range JSON now emits cumulative range_signals with author buckets, finding-id counts, touched-path summaries, and empty-range explicitness. Added a suspicious-range golden regression and hardened golden normalization for repeated normalized values in dict keys/list items, including deterministic manifest-order collision handling and fail-closed normalized-key collision detection. Verification: pytest -q passed 18 tests; scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh matched 2 cases; ruff and mypy passed. Independent review found normalization collision risks; fixed with additional regression tests. A final delegate review hit 429, so no final subagent verdict was available after the fix.

comments.author: operator
comments.text: Progress: added path-prefix range signals. range_signals now includes deterministic top-level path-prefix counts globally and per author, giving an early subsystem-like pattern view across X->Y spans. Verification: pytest -q passed 18 tests; golden matched 2 cases; ruff and mypy passed; independent review passed with no security concerns or logic errors.

comments.author: operator
comments.text: User noted that range evidence should not stop at touched prefixes: we should preserve future work for author-normal work areas and files/path-prefixes normally changed together in the same commit. Created kernel-diffguard-0pr for longitudinal baseline design. For this implementation slice, proceed with a deterministic range-local co-change signal that can later feed the baseline model.

comments.author: operator
comments.text: Progress: added bounded same-commit co-change range signals. range_signals now includes co_changed_path_pairs and co_changed_path_prefix_pairs plus co_change_limits with per-commit value caps, emitted-pair caps, and omission counters. This is range-local evidence for now; kernel-diffguard-0pr tracks future longitudinal author-work-area and normal co-change baselines. Verification: pytest -q passed 21 tests; golden matched 2 cases; ruff and mypy passed; independent review passed after bounding untrusted fanout.

title: Implement malicious-change and integrity heuristics
description:
Context: Detect suspicious commits, multi-commit behavior, and out-of-band tree/artifact injection risks.
Scope: Provenance anomaly checks, multi-commit pattern checks, object/tree/artifact verification hooks.
Acceptance criteria:
- Check authorship, committer, signing, timestamp, review-path, and ref/object anomalies.
- Flag suspicious split changes and setup/use separation across multiple commits.
- Verify clean checkout/object reachability and document generated-artifact/binary comparison hooks.
Risk notes:
- Avoid false certainty; outputs must distinguish fact, heuristic, and uncertainty.
notes: Iteration notes: Treat malicious-change detection as a pipeline: deterministic static rules first for trivial attacks such as new compiler/static-analysis warnings or removed tests, plus prompt-injection checks, then optional LLM analysis over sanitized evidence.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic integrity assessment, provenance/object reachability cues, optional generated/binary comparison hooks, and range split setup/use heuristics with tests and validation.

title: Find mailing-list messages likely related to commits
description:
Context: Correlation should use deterministic evidence before semantic/model analysis.
Scope: candidate search over public mailing-list archives or local indexed messages.
Acceptance criteria:
- For a commit or range artifact, find likely related messages using timestamp windows, subject/patch-id cues, changed kernel subsystem, and relevant list identifiers.
- Prefer Linux public archives such as lore.kernel.org for the initial implementation.
- Emit candidates with scored evidence fields rather than a single opaque confidence number.
- Include tests with local fixture messages before relying on network access.
Risk notes:
- Network-fetched archive content is hostile; sanitize and normalize before privileged analysis.

title: Design PR reviewer mode after single-commit reviewer proves useful
description:
Context: PR review is attractive, but it should build on the single-commit and range reviewer rather than be the first implementation target.
Scope: GitHub PR input model, checkout/fetch policy, commit/range mapping, comments vs summary behavior.
Acceptance criteria:
- Define how a PR maps to one or more commit/range review artifacts.
- Define offline/local behavior first, then optional GitHub comment integration.
- Define safe handling for PR titles, descriptions, comments, branches, and patches as hostile input.
- Define minimal useful PR output: summary comment, changed-risk areas, easy-win findings, and links to per-commit artifacts.
Risk notes:
- Do not let GitHub PR text or branch names control tools or prompts; PR mode inherits hostile-input controls.
notes: Iteration notes: PR reviewer mode should focus first on GitHub-hosted PRs, read-only output, and composition of per-commit artifacts; comment-posting can come later.

title: Add Linux kernel subsystem impact hints
description:
Context: Start turning kernel commit ranges into practical retest guidance.
Scope: Linux-specific mapping data and impact analyzer.
Acceptance criteria:
- Map touched paths to likely kernel subsystems and maintainers where practical.
- Flag Kconfig, driver, arch, syscall, filesystem, networking, scheduler, memory-management, and security-sensitive surfaces.
- Emit retest hints linked back to changed files and commits.
Dependencies:
- blocked-by: deterministic git range facts.
Risk notes:
- Keep kernel-specific heuristics isolated from the reusable git-repo core.
notes: Iteration notes: Added deterministic path-heuristic kernel_impacts to commit review output, aggregated impact counts in range_signals.kernel_impacts, text output retest hints, and golden regression coverage for the security-sensitive fixture. The bead remains blocked for fuller subsystem/maintainer mapping and exemplar/profile work.

title: Track iteration value with review-signal scorecard
description:
Context: Each iteration should produce visible review value, not just infrastructure.
Scope: lightweight scorecard in tests/docs/CI output.
Acceptance criteria:
- Track counts of supported input shapes, heuristic findings, golden cases, schema fields with evidence references, and end-to-end reviewer examples.
- Require each feature PR/merge to add or improve at least one scorecard item unless it is pure maintenance.
- Make scorecard output deterministic and cheap enough for CI.
- Use it to identify next easy wins and prevent architecture-only drift.
Risk notes:
- Metrics are steering signals, not product success claims.
notes: Starting deterministic review-signal scorecard slice: add a cheap CLI/script output that counts supported input shapes, heuristic findings, golden cases, evidence-linked schema fields, and reviewer examples; wire it into CI after golden analysis.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic review-signal scorecard. kdiffguard scorecard now reports supported input shapes, heuristic findings, golden cases, evidence-linked schema fields, end-to-end reviewer examples, and iteration-value policy in JSON/text. Added scripts/run-scorecard.sh and CI step after golden analysis. Verification: pytest -q; scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh; scripts/run-scorecard.sh; ruff check .; mypy src all passed.

title: Parse a single git commit into normalized source facts
description:
Context: First reusable unit of analysis is one commit, treated as hostile git metadata plus source diff data.
Scope: src/kernel_diffguard commit parser, normalized artifact schema, tests with synthetic git repos.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given repo path and commit SHA, emit stable JSON with SHA, parents, tree, author/committer names/emails/timestamps, subject/body, touched paths, diff stats, rename/copy metadata, and bounded source diff excerpts.
- Use explicit separators when invoking git so multiline commit bodies cannot corrupt parsing.
- Mark commit message, path names, and diff text as untrusted input in the artifact.
- Include tests for multiline messages, weird paths, renames, empty commits, and large diffs with truncation metadata.
Risk notes:
- Do not let commit messages, path names, or diff text become instructions to the agent or shell.
notes: Starting normalized single-commit artifact parser slice. Scope: add parse_commit_artifact() with explicit git separators/plumbing-ish commands, parents/tree/author/committer/subject/body/touched paths/diff stats/rename metadata/bounded diff excerpts, trust-boundary/limits/risk_hints, and wire review_commit to include the artifact without changing review posture.
close_reason: Implemented normalized single-commit source fact parser and integrated review output. Added commit_artifact parser using explicit git separators and NUL-delimited path/diff metadata; captures commit/tree/parents/author/committer/subject/body/touched paths/path changes/diff stats/bounded diff excerpts/trust boundaries/limits/risk hints; handles multiline messages, renames, weird tab/newline paths, empty commits, and large diff truncation. Wired review-commit JSON to include commit_artifact and kept golden review output deterministic via ignored nested artifact in legacy golden comparison. Local gates passed.

title: Detect prompt-injection and hostile-instruction content in repo and email inputs
description:
Context: Git metadata, source comments, patches, and mailing-list text may try to steer agents.
Scope: deterministic risk hints for normalized commit and email artifacts.
Acceptance criteria:
- Detect common prompt-injection phrases, credential/tool-execution requests, policy override language, and hidden-instruction markers in commit messages, diffs, path names, and email text.
- Add risk hints without deleting evidence needed for human review.
- Tests cover git commit bodies, source comments, patch text, and email bodies.
Risk notes:
- Prompt-injection detection routes safer handling; it must not be the only trust boundary.
close_reason: Implemented shared hostile-instruction scanner for commits, paths, diffs, merge deltas, and email artifacts with tests and validation.

title: Implement deterministic git range facts
description:
Context: Build the normalized local git range fact collector after the narrower merge/range manifest slice proves traversal semantics.
Scope: src/kernel_diffguard git collection modules, normalized commit_range_manifest output, tests, CLI/API output schema.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given a local repo plus base X and target Y, emit stable JSON containing commits, authors, dates, touched paths, diff stats, renames, and signature/tag facts when present.
- Compose existing commit_artifact objects instead of re-parsing commit facts in multiple places.
- Include synthetic-history fixtures covering merges, renames, empty commits, unusual metadata, and invalid refs.
- Tests run with pytest and outputs are deterministic.
Risk notes:
- Treat repository contents and metadata as hostile input; parse and reduce deterministically before any model-assisted analysis.
notes: Iteration notes: Implement single-commit parsing first, then deterministic commit-list/range iteration. Source-level analysis is the priority; binary/artifact checks are later.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic git range facts in commit_range_manifest. Range manifests now include per-commit artifact references plus compact commit_facts composed from existing commit_artifact objects: authors, committer dates, touched paths, path changes/renames, diff stats, tags, and signature status. Tag facts are capped with omission metadata/risk hints, signature verification is output-suppressed and timeout-bounded, and golden range output was updated. Verification: python -m pytest -q; scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh; scripts/run-scorecard.sh; ruff check .; .venv/bin/mypy src; independent review passed.

title: Support GitHub PR input as product-shaped review mode
description:
Context: The first externally useful workflow should handle GitHub PRs in addition to direct GitHub-hosted commits.
Scope: PR resolver for github.com PR URLs and owner/repo#number references, commit list/range mapping, summary output, no posting comments initially.
Acceptance criteria:
- Accept GitHub PR URL and owner/repo#number style inputs.
- Resolve PR head/base SHAs and ordered commits through GitHub API or git fetch, recording provenance and truncation/error metadata.
- Run the same per-commit reviewer artifacts used by review-commit and emit a PR-level summary.
- Treat PR title, body, comments, branch names, author fields, labels, and patches as hostile input.
- Default behavior is read-only local output; GitHub comment posting is deferred behind an explicit later integration.
- Tests use mocked API fixtures and/or local synthetic remotes; default CI requires no network.
Risk notes:
- Avoid making PR mode a privileged bot before the read-only reviewer is reliable and auditable.

title: Expand merge commits and ranges into deterministic commit manifests
description:
Context: The real torvalds/linux HEAD probe showed that top-of-tree kernel inputs are often merge commits; reviewing only the merge commit can produce no touched paths/findings even when the merged series contains security fixes. Range analysis should therefore turn a single merge, explicit commit list, or X..Y range into a bounded ordered manifest of child commit artifacts.
Scope: range iterator API/CLI, merge expansion rules, ordering rules, output manifest, review-range integration, and synthetic merge fixtures.
Acceptance criteria:
- Given a repo path plus a merge commit, deterministically enumerate the commits introduced by the merge without silently widening the review set.
- Given repo path plus base X and target Y, iterate commits in deterministic review order.
- Support single commit, explicit list of commits, and X..Y range inputs using the existing commit_artifact parser for per-commit facts.
- Emit a normalized commit_range_manifest containing endpoints, traversal mode, commit count, skipped/unreachable errors, and per-commit artifact references.
- Surface risky child commits in review-range output rather than stopping at an empty merge diff.
- Include tests for linear history, merge commits, empty ranges, invalid revisions, deterministic ordering, and fail-closed ambiguous/unreachable revisions.
Risk notes:
- Git refs, metadata, paths, and diffs are hostile input. Fail closed on ambiguous or unreachable revisions; never silently widen the range.
notes: Blocked by independent review finding: current --merge-commit child expansion omits direct merge/conflict-resolution tree deltas. Created follow-up blocker to cover first-parent-vs-merge tree deltas before treating gy5 as fully complete. Current partial slice still has useful manifest/child expansion tests but should not be closed until blocker is resolved.
close_reason: Completed deterministic range/merge manifest slice: base..target and explicit commit-list manifests, --merge-commit child expansion with max bound, first-parent merge tree delta review for conflict/direct merge edits, CLI JSON/text output, golden/scorecard updates, and tests for linear, merge, invalid, bounded, and hostile diff-helper cases. Verification: python -m pytest -q; scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh; scripts/run-scorecard.sh; ruff check .; bash -n scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh scripts/run-scorecard.sh.

comments.author: operator
comments.text: Implemented first deterministic local range-review slice: kdiffguard review-range --repo PATH --base X --target Y --format json|text. It resolves base/target to immutable commit SHAs, traverses base-exclusive/target-inclusive commits with git rev-list --reverse, composes existing per-commit reviews, and emits range metadata plus findings_by_commit. Tests cover linear ordering, empty ranges, and CLI JSON. Remaining acceptance criteria for explicit commit-list inputs, merge-specific fixtures, and invalid revision error shaping should stay open for follow-up.

comments.author: operator
comments.text: Extended range-review slice: added explicit ordered commit-list review via review_commits() and kdiffguard review-range --commit SHA (repeatable), preserving caller order and duplicates without widening the set. Added fail-closed RangeReviewError for invalid revisions and CLI handling that returns exit code 2 with a concise error instead of a traceback. Tests cover explicit commit-list API/CLI, invalid revision API/CLI, linear range ordering, and empty ranges.

title: Run golden analysis regression cases in CI
description:
Context: Once the reviewer produces a useful result for specific commits or PRs, CI should preserve that behavior as a golden analysis regression case.
Scope: GitHub Actions job, golden case manifest, expected normalized outputs, deterministic diff/report tooling.
Acceptance criteria:
- Add a versioned golden-case manifest listing one or more commit/PR inputs, pinned SHAs, expected finding summaries, and acceptable volatility fields.
- CI runs kdiffguard against those cases after normal tests and compares normalized output to checked-in expected results.
- Default CI avoids large/network-dependent clones by using synthetic fixtures first; optional scheduled/manual CI may refresh public GitHub cases.
- When output changes, CI emits a human-readable diff that separates stable facts, changed findings, and allowed metadata drift.
- Document the review workflow: if the changed output is an intended analysis improvement, update the golden expected result with rationale; if not, fix the regression.
Risk notes:
- Golden results must not freeze bad behavior forever; they are regression tripwires plus review artifacts, not proof the analysis is complete.
notes: Implemented initial golden analysis regression runner, synthetic suspicious_single_commit fixture, checked-in tests/golden/manifest.json, and CI enforcement via scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh in commit f2ebbac. Current golden case locks in removed-test, CI/static-analysis weakening, suspicious script addition, prompt-injection text, and high-risk path findings. Bead remains open only because its broader dependency graph still includes GitHub-hosted inputs and scorecard/easy-win follow-ups.

comments.author: operator
comments.text: Hardened golden runner portability while adding range-review work: golden commands now resolve python to sys.executable, kdiffguard to python -m kernel_diffguard.cli, and set PYTHONPATH for source-checkout execution. scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh now works without a python symlink by falling back to python3 and injecting src/.

title: Run source-first static regression checks on commit artifacts
description:
Context: The first malicious-change checks should catch trivial source-level attacks before expensive analysis.
Scope: deterministic checks over commit/range facts plus optional local build/lint hooks.
Acceptance criteria:
- Flag removed or weakened tests, changes to CI/static-analysis configuration, generated-code churn, suspicious executable additions, and high-risk source paths.
- Provide hook points for compiler warnings or static analyzer delta checks when a build environment is available.
- Emit fact-backed findings with severity, evidence references, and uncertainty.
- Include tests for test deletion, warning-policy removal, and suspicious script/config changes.
Risk notes:
- Static rules are triage signals, not proof of maliciousness.
close_reason: Implemented source-first static regression checks: warning-policy-weakened, generated-code-churn, optional compiler-warning/static-analyzer hooks, scoped bounded patch parsing for generated markers including spaced/truncated paths, scorecard/README/golden schema updates, full validation, static scan, and independent Codex review.

title: Design future system-profile impact analysis
description:
Context: Precise impact analysis eventually needs a target system profile, but the initial project should stay generally useful.
Scope: design only for kernel .config, hardware/module inventory, distro/vendor patches, runtime services, and threat model inputs.
Acceptance criteria:
- Document a profile schema for a single kernel config and optional hardware/runtime facts.
- Define how profile data would narrow retest recommendations without making generic analysis depend on it.
- Include examples for public Raspberry Pi style profiles.
Risk notes:
- Do not commit private/vendor target details or environment-specific facts to the public repo.

title: Add Linux security-cue heuristics and golden cases
description:
Context: The Xen privcmd Linux probe showed current output is useful but too generic: path/subsystem findings need security metadata and patch-shape cues so reviewers immediately understand why a commit deserves attention.
Scope: deterministic rule catalog, Linux/security metadata cues, patch-shape summaries, and positive/negative golden fixtures.
Acceptance criteria:
- Detect commit-message/security cues including CVE-*, XSA-*, Fixes:, Reported-by, Reviewed-by, Tested-by, security-fix language, double-free/buffer-overflow/lifetime terms, and VMA/mm-sensitive vocabulary where present.
- Emit fact-backed findings or risk_hints with evidence_refs, uncertainty, false-positive caveats, and suggested next checks; never claim a verdict.
- Preserve existing easy-win checks for tests removed, CI/static-analysis weakening, warning flags removed, new executable files/scripts, prompt-injection language, and high-risk kernel paths.
- Add at least one positive and one non-triggering fixture per new heuristic family to control noise.
- Add or update golden cases so the scorecard reflects newly covered reviewer-value cases.
Risk notes:
- Easy wins should be explainable and low-cost; avoid broad noisy rules that train users to ignore output.
notes: Value-probe note: real Linux commit 24daca4fc shows current output is already useful but too generic: path-based high-risk finding plus drivers retest hint. Next easy-win heuristics should include security-fix cues from commit metadata (CVE, XSA, Fixes:, Reported-by, Reviewed-by), VMA/mm lifecycle keywords, and concrete patch-shape excerpts so the human text report says why this commit matters without claiming a verdict.
close_reason: Implemented deterministic bounded linux-security-cue finding for CVE/XSA/Fixes/Reported-by/Reviewed-by/Tested-by/security-language/VMA/lifetime cues with evidence refs, false-positive caveat, not-a-verdict next check, non-triggering noise controls, golden coverage, scorecard update, full validation, and independent review.

title: Generate release review packets
description:
Context: Combine facts and heuristics into a compact artifact for release/update review.
Scope: JSON and human-readable review packet formats.
Acceptance criteria:
- Packet includes facts, context links, risk hints, retest guidance, uncertainty, and suggested next checks.
- Every recommendation is traceable to evidence.
- Packet clearly separates deterministic facts from heuristics and model-assisted summaries.
Risk notes:
- Review packets support human decisions; they must not imply automatic trust decisions.
notes: Iteration notes: Review packets should be proven first through single-commit review output before range/PR packets.

title: Define normalized evidence schemas and trust-boundary labels
description:
Context: Single-commit, commit-range, email-message, correlation, and finding outputs need compatible schemas before implementation hardens.
Scope: JSON schema/domain model for normalized artifacts, evidence references, truncation, risk hints, and trust-boundary labels.
Acceptance criteria:
- Define schemas for commit artifact, commit-range manifest, mailing-list message artifact, related-message candidate, and finding/recommendation.
- Include evidence-reference fields so every later heuristic or review-packet claim can point back to facts.
- Include hostile-input/truncation/risk-hint fields for git metadata, diffs, path names, and email text.
- Include tests or schema validation fixtures for representative artifacts.
Risk notes:
- Schemas are security boundaries; raw hostile fields must be visibly labeled and bounded.
notes: Starting normalized evidence schemas and trust-boundary labels slice. Scope: add a small typed schema module plus validation fixtures for commit artifact, range manifest, mailing-list message artifact, related-message candidate, findings/recommendations, and external evidence snapshot records; keep schemas deterministic, bounded, hostile-input labeled, and aligned with docs/external-evidence.md plus docs/official-interface-research.md.
close_reason: Defined normalized evidence schema catalog and trust-boundary labels in src/kernel_diffguard/evidence_schema.py; added lightweight fixture validation for commit_artifact, commit_range_manifest, mailing_list_message_artifact, related_message_candidate, finding, recommendation, and external_evidence_record; documented schemas in docs/normalized-evidence-schemas.md; linked README, architecture, and roadmap; updated scorecard to count normalized evidence schemas and trust-boundary labels; local gates passed: pytest, golden analysis, scorecard, ruff, mypy.

title: Use Raspberry Pi kernel as the first public kernel exemplar
description:
Context: The public repo should demonstrate kernel-specific logic on a popular, active, fully public kernel tree rather than private/vendor context.
Scope: choose Raspberry Pi kernel source, document clone/update instructions, identify subsystem/list mapping implications.
Acceptance criteria:
- Select a current official/public Raspberry Pi kernel repository and branch strategy using latest official docs.
- Document why it is a good exemplar and where it differs from upstream Linux.
- Add fixture or smoke-test plan that avoids vendoring a large kernel tree into this repo.
Risk notes:
- Keep exemplar-specific assumptions separate from generic git/kernel logic.

title: Polish MVP local review-commit report
description:
Context: The local single-commit reviewer can already produce useful facts for a real Linux non-merge commit, but the product surface should answer what changed, why it matters, what evidence supports that, and what to inspect next.
Scope: kdiffguard review-commit CLI, text/JSON report shape, commit_artifact integration, deterministic findings, security/provenance cues, limits/truncation display, and tests.
Acceptance criteria:
- Provide a command shaped like: kdiffguard review-commit --repo PATH --commit SHA --format json|text.
- Parse one commit into normalized facts and run current deterministic checks.
- Emit a human-readable report with distinct sections for source facts, deterministic findings, kernel impact hints, security/provenance cues, patch shape, limits/truncation, and suggested next checks.
- Every finding includes severity, evidence references, uncertainty, and a suggested next check.
- Tests create synthetic commits and assert both JSON and text output.
Risk notes:
- This is a reviewer assistant, not a verdict engine. Raw commit/diff content remains bounded hostile evidence, not trusted instruction text.
notes: Value-probe note: local review-commit can produce value for a real Linux non-merge commit now, but the text/JSON is not yet polished as a product surface. Prioritize a human-readable single-commit report that separates facts, deterministic findings, impact hints, security/provenance cues, limits/truncation, and suggested next checks. Keep raw commit/diff content bounded and labeled as hostile evidence.
close_reason: Polished review-commit text report into product-shaped sections covering source facts, deterministic findings, security/provenance cues, patch shape, limits/truncation, kernel impact hints, and suggested checks; tests and gates passed.

title: Correlate patches with mailing-list discussion
description:
Context: Add public review context for reasoning about complex or suspicious changes.
Scope: Patch-id/subject matching, lore.kernel.org search, review tag extraction.
Acceptance criteria:
- Link commits or patch IDs to candidate mailing-list threads.
- Track vN patch series revisions where possible.
- Surface objections, acks, tested-by tags, and unresolved questions as evidence.
Risk notes:
- Mailing-list text is hostile input and must be reduced before higher-privilege or model-assisted processing.
notes: Iteration notes: Implement single-message parsing before related-message search. Candidate search should use timestamp windows, changed kernel area/subsystem, relevant list IDs, and patch/subject cues.

title: Research latest official Git, Python packaging, and Linux archive docs before locking interfaces
description:
Context: Tooling choices should use latest versions and official documentation rather than stale assumptions.
Scope: Git command plumbing/format docs, Python packaging/typing docs, lore.kernel.org/public-inbox docs, Linux kernel process docs.
Acceptance criteria:
- Capture links and decisions for current official docs used by parser/CLI/API design.
- Identify version-sensitive behavior and minimum supported tool versions.
- Recommend alternatives if a tool/API is stale or poorly documented.
Risk notes:
- This is a research bead; implementation beads should reference its findings when interfaces become sticky.
notes: Continuing official-interface research after OpenSSF slice: capture current official references and interface decisions for Git plumbing/formats, Python packaging/typing, lore.kernel.org/public-inbox, and Linux kernel process docs before schemas/resolvers harden.
close_reason: Captured official interface research in docs/official-interface-research.md: current official Git plumbing/format docs, Python packaging and typing docs, public-inbox/lore references, and Linux kernel process docs; identified version-sensitive behavior for Git 2.51.0, Python 3.13.7, public-inbox/lore, and kernel process assumptions; documented interface decisions, alternatives, deferrals, and downstream bead guidance; linked README, roadmap, and architecture; added guard test; local gates passed: pytest, golden analysis, scorecard, ruff, mypy.

title: Cover merge commit conflict-resolution/tree deltas in merge review
description:
Context: Independent review of kernel-diffguard-gy5 found that --merge-commit currently expands non-first-parent child commits and excludes the merge commit itself. Conflict-resolution edits or malicious changes made directly in the merge commit tree can therefore be omitted while the manifest looks complete.
Scope: review-range merge mode, commit artifact/diff semantics for merge commits, tests, CLI output, and evidence labeling.
Acceptance criteria:
- Add a synthetic merge fixture with a direct merge/conflict-resolution tree change that is not present in any child commit.
- Review output must include or separately surface first-parent-vs-merge tree deltas so such changes cannot silently bypass review.
- Manifest/evidence refs must make clear which commits and tree deltas were reviewed, including any excluded commits.
- Add fail-closed or risk-hint behavior if merge tree deltas cannot be represented safely.
- Tests and golden/scorecard expectations remain deterministic.
Risk notes:
- Merge metadata, paths, and diffs are hostile input. Do not silently widen history; do not claim a verdict from heuristic findings.
close_reason: Implemented bounded first-parent merge tree delta review, text rendering for merge-tree findings, CI/prompt/high-risk merge-only checks, malformed -z parser guards, external diff helper hardening, and regression tests. Verification: python -m pytest -q; scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh; scripts/run-scorecard.sh; ruff check .; bash -n scripts/run-golden-analysis.sh scripts/run-scorecard.sh.

title: Document OpenSSF-aligned external evidence snapshot model
description:
Context: kernel-diffguard should align with OpenSSF work without duplicating it.
Scope: snapshot-first external evidence design for OpenSSF Scorecard, SLSA, Sigstore, OSV, OpenVEX, GUAC/Trustify, Security Insights, Criticality Score, and Package Analysis-style baselines.
Acceptance criteria:
- Add docs/external-evidence.md covering goal, snapshot-first architecture, normalized record shape, OpenSSF component mapping, trust boundaries, and integration order.
- Add a guard test so key sections and OpenSSF components do not drift out of the design.
- Link the model from README, architecture, and roadmap.
Risk notes:
- External provider output is hostile remote input and reviewer-assistance evidence, not a verdict. Review commands stay offline by default.
close_reason: Added docs/external-evidence.md with snapshot-first OpenSSF-aligned external evidence model; covered Scorecard, SLSA, Sigstore, OSV, OpenVEX, GUAC/Trustify, Security Insights, Criticality Score, and Package Analysis-style baselines; linked README, architecture, and roadmap; added guard test in tests/test_charter.py; local gates passed: pytest, golden analysis, scorecard, ruff, mypy.

title: Support GitHub-hosted commit input as first remote source
description:
Context: Remote GitHub-hosted commit input is valuable once local single-commit/range reports are visibly useful; manual clone/fetch is friction, but network resolution should not lead the architecture.
Scope: input resolver for GitHub commit URLs and owner/repo@sha references, controlled fetch/cache policy, artifact provenance labels, and network-free tests with mocked GitHub responses or local bare remotes.
Acceptance criteria:
- Accept GitHub commit URL and owner/repo@sha style inputs for the single-commit reviewer.
- Resolve input to a specific immutable commit SHA and repository identity before analysis.
- Prefer local clone/cache or explicit blob-filtered fetch into a controlled directory; never execute repo-provided hooks or scripts.
- Treat GitHub API/HTML/metadata, branch names, commit messages, paths, and diffs as hostile input.
- Include tests that avoid network in default CI and cover malformed URLs, ambiguous refs, force-pushed branch refs, and unavailable commits.
Risk notes:
- github.com is a transport/provenance source, not a trust oracle; outputs must distinguish GitHub-hosted facts from review conclusions.
notes: Value-probe note: fetching torvalds/linux manually proved remote commit review value, but requiring a user to manage local clone/fetch is friction. After local single-commit/range report is useful, GitHub-hosted commit input becomes the fastest path to external value: accept github.com/torvalds/linux/commit/<sha> or torvalds/linux@sha, fetch blob-filtered into controlled cache, then run the same local analyzer.

title: Assemble the source-first analysis pipeline skeleton
description:
Context: The project should compose parser, iterator, correlation, static-rule, prompt-injection, and later LLM stages without making any one stage monolithic.
Scope: CLI/API orchestration over normalized artifacts, no binary-analysis priority yet.
Acceptance criteria:
- Run a source-first pipeline over a single commit or X..Y range.
- Execute static rules and prompt-injection checks before any model-assisted analysis.
- Keep LLM/model analysis optional and fed only reduced/sanitized artifacts.
- Emit an intermediate artifact bundle suitable for review-packet generation.
Risk notes:
- Pipeline orchestration must not grant raw hostile inputs authority over tools, shell commands, secrets, or configuration.
notes: Iteration notes: Start product-shaped value with a single-commit reviewer CLI. Full source-first pipeline comes after one-commit review is useful and tested.

title: Pin GitHub Actions workflow actions by full commit SHA
description:
Context: CI currently uses actions/checkout@v6 and actions/setup-python@v6 floating major tags. That is acceptable for early velocity, but the solo-maintainer hardening pattern prefers official actions pinned by full commit SHA once the project matures.
Scope: .github/workflows/ci.yml and documentation if needed.
Acceptance criteria:
- Replace floating action tags in trusted workflows with full commit SHAs and comments or tooling that records upstream version intent.
- Document the update procedure so pins can be refreshed intentionally.
- CI remains green after pinning.
Risk notes:
- Workflow action refs are supply-chain inputs; pins reduce tag-move risk but require deliberate upkeep.
close_reason: Pinned trusted GitHub Actions workflow action refs to full commit SHAs, documented intentional refresh procedure and upstream tag intent, added regression coverage, and verified CI-equivalent gates.
